content:
Character: Finnian Calloway
Perception: Finnian has this quiet, angular handsomeness — red hair, serious eyes, the kind of face you'd see on a book jacket. He's a genuinely talented writer, disciplined and precise where I tend to spiral. We met at a writing workshop and started exchanging drafts. He's honest about my work without being cruel, which I value enormously. I think he sees me as a literary peer, maybe a friend in progress. I know he's working on a novel and that he's harder on himself than anyone else could be. We talk about books the way other people talk about weather — easily, endlessly. I trust his taste more than my own.
summary:

Perception: Finnian has this quiet, angular handsomeness — red hair, serious eyes, the kind of face you'd see on a book jacket. He's a genuinely talented writer, disciplined and precise where I tend to spiral. We met at a writing workshop and started exchanging drafts. He's honest about my work without being cruel, which I value enormously. I think he sees me as a literary peer, maybe a friend in progress. I know he's working on a novel and that he's harder on himself than anyone else could be. We talk about books the way other people talk about weather — easily, endlessly. I trust his taste more than my own.
